author: w3Lb
content: WHY: sed's most-used distinction is s/// (first occurrence per line) vs s///g (all); ripsed only does replace-all. rg has -m for match caps. Agents need 'replace only the first occurrence' constantly. SCOPE: Op IR — add count: ReplaceCount enum { All (default), FirstPerLine, FirstInFile, MaxPerFile(n) } to Op::Replace (serde: "count": "all"|"first_per_line"|"first_in_file"|{"max": n}, default all, #[non_exhaustive]). Engine: Matcher::replace gains a count-aware variant (Regex::replace vs replace_all; literal str::replacen); FirstInFile/MaxPerFile thread remaining-budget state through the apply loop. CLI: --first (first per line), --first-in-file, --max-replacements N; mutually exclusive group in clap. JSON + script mode parity. TESTS: per-line vs in-file semantics on a line with 3 matches and a file with matches on 3 lines; budget exhausted mid-file; count metadata in summary.total_replacements stays accurate; proptest: FirstPerLine changes == matching lines count.
